Alternatively, you may choose up to two races of humanoids as your favored enemy. You have advantage on Survival (WIS) checks to track your favored enemy, as well as INT checks to recall information about them. You may learn one language that is spoken by your favored enemy, if they speak one.

content.name: Favored Foe (Optional)
content.desc: This replaces the Favored Enemy trait if chosen. When you hit a creature with an attack, you may mark that target as your favored enemy for 1 minute. The first time on each of your turns that you hit the favored enemy and deal damage to it, including when you mark it, you  may add 1d4 to the damage roll. You may use this trait to mark an enemy an amount equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all expended uses during a long rest.

1.name: Natural Explorer
1.desc: Choose a type of favored terrain: arctic, coast, desert, forest, grassland, mountain, swamp or the Underdark. When you make an INT or WIS check related to your favored terrain, your proficiency bonus is doubled if you're using a proficient skill. Additionally, when traveling for an hour or more in your favored terrain, you gain the following benefits:

content.desc: Difficult terrain doesn't slow your group's travel.
Your group cannot become lost except by magical means.
Even when you are engaged in another activity while traveling, you remain alert to danger.
If you are traveling alone, you can move stealthily at a normal pace.
When you forage, you find twice as much food as you would normally.
While tracking other creatures, you also learn their exact number, sizes and how long ago they passed through the area.

content.name: Deft Explorer (Optional)
content.desc: This replaces the Natural Explorer trait if chosen. You gain the following trait:

content.name: Canny
content.desc: Choose one of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using the chosen skill. Additionally, you learn to speak, read and write 2 languages of your choice.

2.name: Spellcasting
2.desc: You have learned to use the magical essence of nature to cast spells, much as a druid does.

content.name: Known Spells
content.desc: The Spells Known column of the ranger spell slot table shows how many ranger spells you know at any given level. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you may choose one of the spells you know, and replace it with any other ranger spell. Each spell you learn can only be of a spell slot level you have.

content.name: Spellcasting Ability
content.desc: Your spellcasting ability is WIS.

content.name: Spell Save DC
content.desc: 8 + proficiency + WIS

content.name: Spell attack modifier
content.desc: proficiency + WIS

2.name: Fighting Style
2.desc: Choose one of the following. You cannot take a style option more than once, even if you get to choose again. You may replace your fighting style with a different one if you gain a level in this class.

content.name: Archery
content.desc: You gain +2 to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

content.name: Blind fighting
content.desc: You have blindsight with a range of 10ft.

content.name: Defense
content.desc: While you are wearing armor, you gain +1 to AC.

content.name: Druidic Warrior
content.desc: You learn 2 cantrips of your choice from the druid spell list. They count as ranger spells for you. WIS is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you may replace one of these cantrips with another from the druid spell list.

content.name: Dueling
content.desc: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain +2 to damage rolls with that weapon.

content.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
content.desc: You may draw a weapon that has the thrown property as part of the attack you make with that weapon. Additionally, you gain +2 to damage if you hit a ranged thrown weapon attack.

content.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
content.desc: When you engage in two-weapon fighting, you may add your ability modifier to the damage of the second attack.

3.name: Ranger Conclave
3.desc: Choose a subclass:

3.name: Primeval Awareness
3.desc: You may use your action and expend one ranger spell slot to focus your awareness on the region around you. For 1 minute per level of the spell slot you expend, you can sense whether the following types of creatures are present within 1 mile of you (increased to 6 miles in your favored terrain): aberrations, celestials, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends and undead. This feature doesn't reveal the location or number of creatures.

content.name: Primal Awareness (Optional)
content.desc: This replaces the Primeval Awareness trait when chosen. You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, if you don't already know them. At 3rd level, you learn the speak with animals spell. This spell doesn't count towards the number of ranger spells you can know.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.desc: You may attack twice instead of once whenever you take the @attack action on your turn.

5.name: Primal Awareness (Optional)
5.desc: You only have this trait if you took the Primal Awareness trait at 3rd level. You learn the @beast sense spell. This spell doesn't count towards the number of ranger spells you can know.

6.name: Favored Enemy
6.desc: You may choose an additional type of favored enemy, for a total of 2.

content.name: Favored Foe (Optional)
content.desc: You only have this trait if you took the Favored Foe trait at 1st level. The favored foe mark damage increases to 1d6.

6.name: Natural Explorer
6.desc: You may choose an additional type of favored terrain, for a total of 2.

content.name: Deft Explorer (Optional)
content.desc: You only have this trait if you took the Deft Explorer trait at 1st level. You gain the following trait.

content.name: Roving
content.desc: Your walking speed increases by 5ft., and you gain a climbing and swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

7.name: Ranger Conclave Feature
7.desc: You gain a feature depending on your ranger conclave.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

8.name: Land's Stride
8.desc: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed or damaged by them. You have advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created and/or manipulated to impede movement.

9.name: Primal Awareness (Optional)
9.desc: You only have this trait if you took the Primal Awareness trait at 3rd level. You learn the @speak with plants spell. This spell doesn't count towards the number of ranger spells you can know.

10.name: Natural Explorer
10.desc: You may choose an additional type of favored terrain, for a total of 3.

content.name: Deft Explorer (Optional)
content.desc: You only have this trait if you took the Deft Explorer trait at 1st level. You gain the following trait.

content.name: Tireless
content.desc: As an action, you may give yourself a number of temporary hitpoints equal to 1d8 + your WIS, minimum of 1 hitpoint. You may do this an amount of times equal to your proficiency bonus. Additionally, a short rest decreases your exhaustion level by 1.

11.name: Ranger Conclave Feature
11.desc: You gain a feature depending on your ranger conclave.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

13.name: Primal Awareness (Optional)
13.desc: You only have this trait if you took the Primal Awareness trait at 3rd level. You learn the @locate creature spell. This spell doesn't count towards the number of ranger spells you can know.

14.name: Favored Enemy
14.desc: You may choose an additional type of favored enemy, for a total of 3.

content.name: Favored Foe (Optional)
content.desc: You only have this trait if you took the Favored Foe trait at 1st level. The favored foe mark damage increases to 1d8.

15.name: Ranger Conclave Feature
15.desc: You gain a feature depending on your ranger conclave.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

17.name: Primal Awareness (Optional)
17.desc: You only have this trait if you took the Primal Awareness trait at 3rd level. You learn the @commune with nature spell. This spell doesn't count towards the number of ranger spells you can know.

18.name: Feral Senses
18.desc: When you attack a creature you cannot see, your inability to see it doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack rolls. Additionally, you gain 30ft. of blindsense.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

20.name: Foe Slayer
20.desc: Once on each of your turns, you can add your WIS modifier to the attack roll or damage roll of an attack you make against one of your favored enemies (or marked as favored if you have the Favored Foe trait). You may choose to use this feature before or after the roll, but before any effects of the roll are applied.

rogue.name: Rogue
desc: Rogues rely on skill, stealth, and their foes' vulnerabilities to get the upper hand in any situation. They have a knack for finding the solution to just about any problem, demonstrating a resourcefulness and versatility that is the cornerstone of any successful adventuring party.
equipment: a burglar's pack, a dungeoneer's pack
leather armor, 2 daggers, and thieves' tools

1.name: Sneak Attack
1.desc: You may deal damage to 1 creature if you attack that creature with advantage. This must be with a finesse or ranged weapon. You don't need advantage if another creature is within 5ft. of it, that enemy isn't incapacitated, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll. Sneak attack deals damage equal to 1d6 * half your rogue level (rounded up).

1.name: Expertise
1.desc: You may choose 2 of your chosen skill proficiencies, or one of your skill proficiencies and your proficiency with thieves' tools. These 2 proficiencies will have doubled proficiency bonus.

1.name: Thieves' Cant
1.desc: You learn Thieves' Cant, a mixture of jargon and code woven through spoken language. This allows you to speak this language to others who have learned this secret language, conveying secret messages. Conveying a message in this way takes 4x as long.

2.name: Cunning Action
2.desc: You may use a bonus action on each of your combat turns. This bonus action may only be used on the @dash, @disengage or @hide action.

3.name: Roguish Archetype
3.desc: Choose a subclass:

3.name: Steady Aim
3.desc: You may give yourself advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn as a bonus action. You may only do this if you haven't moved this turn, and your speed will be reduced to 0ft. until the start of your next turn once you do.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

5.name: Uncanny Dodge
5.desc: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you may use your reaction to halve the attack's damage.

6.name: Expertise
6.desc: You may choose an additional 2 skills and gain expertise in them.

7.name: Evasion
7.desc: When you are forced to make a DEX saving throw to take only half damage from an area attack, you instead take no damage if you succeed the saving throw, and half damage if you fail.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

9.name: Roguish Archetype Feature
9.desc: You gain a feature depending on your roguish archetype.

10.name: Ability Score Improvement
10.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

11.name: Reliable Talent
11.desc: Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you may treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

13.name: Roguish Archetype Feature
13.desc: You gain a feature depending on your roguish archetype.

14.name: Blindsense
14.desc: You gain 10ft. of blindsense.

15.name: Slippery Mind
15.desc: You gain proficiency in WIS saving throws.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

17.name: Roguish Archetype Feature
17.desc: You gain a feature depending on your roguish archetype.

18.name: Elusive
18.desc: As long as you are not incapacitated, no attack roll has advantage against you.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

20.name: Stroke of Luck
20.desc: If your attack misses a target within range, you may turn the miss into a hit. Alternatively, if you fail an ability check, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20. You may use this trait once every long rest.

sorcerer.name: Sorcerer
desc: Sorcerers carry a magical birthright conferred upon them by an exotic bloodline, some otherworldly influence, or exposure to unknown cosmic forces. No one chooses sorcery; the power chooses the sorcerer.

1.name: Sorcerous Origin
1.desc: Choose a subclass:

1.name: Spellcasting
1.desc: An event in your past, or in the life of a parent or ancestor, left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with arcane magic. This font of magic, whatever its origins, fuels your spells.

content.name: Cantrips
content.desc: You know 4 cantrips of your choice from the sorcerer spell list.

content.name: Known Spells
content.desc: The Spells Known column of the sorcerer spell slot table shows how many sorcerer spells you know at any given level. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you may choose one of the spells you know, and replace it with any other sorcerer spell. Each spell you learn can only be of a spell slot level you have.

content.name: Spellcasting Focus
content.desc: You may use an arcane focus as a spellcasting focus for your sorcerer spells.

content.name: Spellcasting Ability
content.desc: Your spellcasting ability is CHA.

content.name: Spell Save DC
content.desc: 8 + proficiency + CHA

content.name: Spell attack modifier
content.desc: proficiency + CHA

2.name: Font of Magic
2.desc: You tap into a deep wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by sorcery points, which allow you to create a variety of magical effects.

content.name: Sorcery Points
content.desc: You have an amount of sorcery points equal to your sorcerer level. You may never have more sorcery points than your sorcerer level at any one time. You regain all spent sorcery points after a long rest.

content.name: Flexible Casting
content.desc: You may use your sorcery points to gain additional spell slots, or sacrifice spell slots to gain additional sorcery points.

content.name: Creating Spell Slots
content.desc: You may transform unexpended sorcery points into a spell slot level as a bonus action on your turn. The cost increases with the spell slot level: 2 sorcery points for a 1st level spell slot, 3 points for 2nd level, 5 points for 3rd level, 6 points for 4th level and 7 points for 5th level. You cannot create spell slots of 6th level or higher. Any spell slot you create with this feature vanishes after a long rest.

content.name: Converting a Spell Slot to Sorcery Points
content.desc: As a bonus action on your turn, you may expend one spell slot, and gain a number of sorcery points equal to the spell slot's level.

3.name: Metamagic
3.desc: You gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You gain 2 of the following Metamagic options of your choice. You may only use one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless otherwise noted.

content.name: Careful Spell
content.desc: Whenever you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you may protect some of those creature's from the spell's full force. You may spend 1 sorcery point and choose a number of creatures up to your CHA (minimum of 1). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell.

content.name: Distant Spell
content.desc: Whenever you cast a spell that has a range of 5ft. or greater, you may spend 1 sorcery point to double the range of the spell. Alternatively, if the spell has a range of touch, you may spend 1 sorcery point to make the range of the spell 30ft.

content.name: Empowered Spell
content.desc: Whenever you roll for damage for a spell, you may spend 1 sorcery point to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your CHA (minimum of 1). You must use the new rolls. You may use this Metamagic option even if you already used a different one during the casting of the spell.

content.name: Extended Spell
content.desc: Whenever you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you may spend 1 sorcery point to double its duration, to a maximum duration of 24 hours.

content.name: Heightened Spell
content.desc: Whenever you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you may spend 3 sorcery points to give one target of the spell disadvantage on its first saving throw made against the spell.

content.name: Quickened Spell
content.desc: Whenever you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action, you may spend 2 sorcery points to change the casting time to 1 bonus action for this casting.

content.name: Seeking Spell
content.desc: If you make an attack roll for a spell and miss, you may spend 2 sorcerer points to reroll the d20, and you must use the new roll. You may use this Metamagic option even if you already used a different one during the casting of the spell.

content.name: Subtle Spell
content.desc: Whenever you cast a spell, you may spend 1 sorcery point to cast it without any somatic or verbal components.

content.name: Transmuted Spell
content.desc: Whenever you cast a spell that deals acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison or thunder damage, you may spend 1 sorcery point to change that damage type to any of the listed types.

content.name: Twinned Spell
content.desc: Whenever you cast a spell that targets only 1 creature and does not have a range of self, you may spend a number of sorcery points equal to the spell's level to target a second creature in range with the same spell. You spend 1 sorcery points if the spell is a cantrip. To be eligible, a spell must be incapable of targeting more that one creature at the spell's current level.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

4.name: Cantrips
4.desc: You learn an additional cantrip, for a total of 5.

5.name: Magical Guidance
5.desc: When you make an ability check that fails, you may spend 1 sorcery point to reroll the d20, and you must use the new roll, potentially turning the failure into a success.

6.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
6.desc: You gain a feature depending on your sorcerous origin.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

10.name: Metamagic
10.desc: You gain an additional Metamagic option of your choice, for a total of 3.

10.name: Cantrips
10.desc: You learn an additional cantrip, for a total of 6.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

14.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
14.desc: You gain a feature depending on your sorcerous origin.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

17.name: Metamagic
17.desc: You gain an additional Metamagic option of your choice, for a total of 4.

18.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
18.desc: You gain a feature depending on your sorcerous origin.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

20.name: Sorcerous Restoration
20.desc: When you roll initiative and have no uses of your bardic inspiration trait left, you regain one use.

warlock.name: Warlock
desc: Warlocks are seekers of the knowledge that lies hidden in the fabric of the multiverse. Through pacts made with mysterious beings of supernatural power, warlocks unlock magical effects both subtle and spectacular.
equipment: leather armor, any simple weapon, and 2 daggers

1.name: Otherworldly Patron
1.desc: Choose a subclass:

1.name: Pact Magic
1.desc: Your arcane research and the magic bestowed upon you by your patron have given you facility with spells.

content.name: Cantrips
content.desc: You know 2 cantrips of your choice from the warlcok spell list.

content.name: Spell Slots
content.desc: The warlock spell slot table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your warlock spells of 1st to 5th level. All your spell slots are the same level. You regain all expended spell slots after a short rest.

content.name: Known Spells
content.desc: The Spells Known column of the warlock spell slot table shows how many warlock spells you know at any given level. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you may choose one of the spells you know, and replace it with any other warlock spell. Each spell you learn can only be of a spell slot level you have.

content.name: Spellcasting Focus
content.desc: You may use an arcane focus as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells.

content.name: Spellcasting Ability
content.desc: Your spellcasting ability is CHA.

content.name: Spell Save DC
content.desc: 8 + proficiency + CHA

content.name: Spell attack modifier
content.desc: proficiency + CHA

2.name: Eldritch Invocations
2.desc: You gain 2 eldritch invocations of your choice. You may replace any one existing invocation you know with a different one whenever you gain a level in this class. The following invocations become available to you:

content.name: Agonizing Blast
content.desc: Prerequisite: @eldritch blast. When you cast @eldritch blast, you may add your CHA modifier to the damage roll.

content.name: Armor of Shadows
content.desc: You may cast the @mage armor spell on yourself at will without expending a spell slot or needing material components.

content.name: Aspect of the Moon
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the tome. You no longer need sleep, and cannot be forced to sleep by any means. To gain the benefits of a long rest, you may spend all 8 hours doing light activity, such as reading or keeping watch.

content.name: Beast Speech
content.desc: You may cast the @speak with animals spell at will without expending a spell slot.

content.name: Beguiling Influence
content.desc: You gain proficiency in Deception (CHA) and Persuasion (CHA).

content.name: Book of Ancient Secrets
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the tome. You can now inscribe magical rituals into your book of shadows. You may choose 2 1st-level spells of any class that have the ritual tag. These spell appear in your book, and do not count agains the number of spells you own. With your book in hand, you may cast the chosen spells as rituals. You cannot cast these spells unless as rituals, unless you've learned them by other means. You may also cast other warlock spells you know as rituals if they have the ritual tag. You may add other ritual spells into your book, provided the spell's level is equal to or less than half your warlock level (rounded up). Transcribing costs 2 hours and 50gp for each level of the spell.

content.name: Devil's Sight
content.desc: You can see normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, up to 120ft.

content.name: Eldritch Mind
content.desc: You gain advantage to CON saving throws you make to maintain concentration on a spell.

content.name: Eldritch Sight
content.desc: You may cast the @detect magic spell at will, without expending a spell slot or needing material components.

content.name: Eldritch Spear
content.desc: Prerequisite: @eldritch blast. The range of your @eldritch blast is 300ft.

content.name: Eyes of the Rune Keeper
content.desc: You can read all writing.

content.name: Fiendish Vigor
content.desc: You may cast the @false life spell on yourself at will, as a 1st-level spell, without expending a spell slot or needing material components.

content.name: Gift of the Ever-Living Ones
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the chain. Spells and effects that restore your hitpoints restore the maximum amount, as long as your familiar is within 100ft. of you.

content.name: Grasp of Hadar
content.desc: Prerequisite: @eldritch blast. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with your @eldritch blast, you may move that creature 10ft. in a straight line closer to yourself

content.name: Improved Pact Weapon
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the blade. You may use any weapon you summon with your pact of the blade feature as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells. Additionally, the weapon gains +1 to attack and damage rulles, unless it already gains these bonuses from being magical. The weapon may also be a shortbow, longbow, light crossbow or heavy crossbow.

content.name: Investment of the Chain Master
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the chain. When you cast @find familiar, you infuse the summoned familiar with a measure of your eldritch power, granting the creature the following benefits:
The familiar gains either a flying or swimming speed of 40ft. (your choice).
As a bonus action, you may command the familiar to take the @attack action.
The familiar's weapon attacks are considered magical.
If the familiar forces a creature to make a saving throw, it uses your spell save DC.
Whenever the familiar takes damage, you may use your reaction to halve the damage.

content.name: Lance of Lethargy
content.desc: Prerequisite: @eldritch blast. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with your @eldritch blast, you may reduce the target's speed by 10ft. until the end of your next turn.

content.name: Mask of Many Faces
content.desc: You may cast the @disguise self spell at will, without expending a spell slot.

content.name: Misty Visions
content.desc: You may cast the @silent image spell at will without expending a spell slot, or needing material components.

content.name: Rebuke of the Talisman
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the talisman. Whenever the wearer of your talisman is hit by an attacker you can see within 30ft. of you, you may use your reaction to deal @psychic damage to the attacker equal to your proficiency bonus, and push it up to 10ft. away from the wearer.

content.name: Repelling Blast
content.desc: Prerequisite: @eldritch blast. Whenever you hit a creature with @eldritch blast, you may push the creature up to 10ft. in a straight line away from you.

content.name: Thief of Five Fates
content.desc: You may cast the @bane spell using a warlock spell slot, once every long rest.

content.name: Voice of the Chain Master
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the chain. You may communicate telepathically with your familiar and perceive through your familiar's senses as long as you are on the same plane of existence. While perceiving through your familiar's senses, you may speak through your familiar in your own voice, even if your familiar is normally incapable of speech.

3.name: Pact Boon
3.desc: Your patron bestows upon you a gift for your service. Choose one of the following:

content.desc: You may use your action to create a pact weapon in an empty hand. You may choose the form of this weapon. You are proficient with it while you wield it. This weapon counts as magical.

Your pact weapon will disappear if it is more than 5ft. away from you for 1 minute. It also disappears if you use this feature to create a new weapon, if you dismiss the weapon (no action required), or if you die.

You may transform one magic weapon into your pact weapon by performing a special ritual while you hold the weapon. You perform this ritual over the course of 1 hour, which can be done during a short rest. After this, you may dismiss the weapon and recall it willingly. You may not affect an artifact or sentient weapon in this way. The weapon ceases being your pact weapon if you die, perform the ritual on a different weapon or use the same ritual to break the bond. If the weapon was dismissed while the bond breaks, it appears at your feet.
You learn the @find familiar spell and may cast it as a ritual. This spell doesn't count against your number of spells known.
When you cast the spell, you may choose one of the normal forms for your familiar, or one of the following: imp, pseudodragon, quasit, sprite.
When you take the @attack action, you may forego one of your own attacks to grant your familiar the ability to make one attack, using its reaction instead.

Your patron grants you the Book of Shadows. When you gain this feature, you may choose any 3 cantrips from any class's spell list. While the book is on your person, you may cast those cantrips at will. They don't count against your number of cantrips known. If they don't appear on the warlock's spell list, they are nonetheless warlock spells for you.

You may create a new Book of Shadows. You must perform a 1 hour ritual to receive a replacement from your patron. The new book may contain new cantrips. The current book turns to ash if you die, or if a new book is granted.

Your patron grants you an amulet. When the wearer fails an ability check, they may add 1d4 to the roll, potentially turning the failure into a success. This benefit can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus every long rest.

Should you lose the amulet, you may perform a 1 hour ritual to receive a replacement from your patron. The current amulet turns to dust if you die, or if you are granted a new amulet through the ritual.

3.name: Cantrips
3.desc: You learn an additional cantrip, for a total of 3.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

5.name: Eldritch Invocations
5.desc: You gain an additional eldritch invocation, for a total of 3. The following invocations become available to you:

content.name: Cloak of Flies
content.desc: As a bonus action, you may surround yourself with a magical aura resembling buzzing flies. The aura extends 5ft. from you in every direction, but not through total cover, lasting until you're incapacitated or until you dismiss it as a bonus action. While the aura persists, you gain advantage on Intimidation (CHA) checks, but disadvantage on all other CHA checks. Any creature that starts its turn in the aura takes @poison damage equal to your CHA (minimum of 0). You may use this invocation once every short rest.

content.name: Eldritch Smite
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the blade. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you may expend a warlock spell slot to deal an extra 1d8 + 1d8 per spell slot level @force damage to the target. If the target is Huge or smaller, you may knock it prone.

content.name: Far Scribe
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the tome. A new page appears in your book of shadows. With your permission, a creature may use its action to write its name on that page, up to a number of names equal to your proficiency bonus. You may cast the @sending spell at will without expending a spell slot, or requiring material components, targeting a creature whose name is on the page. The target hears the message in their mind, and if the target replies, their message appears on the page, rather than in your mind, disappearing again after 1 minute. You may erase a name on the page as an action, by touching it.

content.name: Gift of the Depths
content.desc: You may breathe underwater, and you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. You may cast the @water breathing spell without expending a spell slot, once per long rest.

content.name: Maddening Hex
content.desc: Prerequisite: the @hex spell, or a warlock feature that curses. As a bonus action, you cause a psychic disturbance around the target cursed by your spell or effect. When you do so, you deal @psychic damage equal to your CHA (minimum of 1) to the cursed target and all creatures of your choice within 5ft. of it. You may use this invocation only if it's a target you can see within 30ft. of you.

content.name: Mire the Mind
content.desc: You may cast the @slow spell using a warlock spell slot, once every long rest.

content.name: One With Shadows
content.desc: When you are in an area of dim light or darkness, you may use your action to become invisible until you move or take an action or reaction.

content.name: Sign of Ill Omen
content.desc: You may cast the @bestow curse spell using a warlock spell slot, once per long rest.

content.name: Thirsting Blade
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the blade. You may attack with your pact weapon twice instead of once, whenever you take the @attack action on your turn.

content.name: Tomb of Levistus
content.desc: As a reaction when you take damage, you may entomb yourself in ice, which melts away at the end of your next turn. You gain 10 temporary hitpoints per warlock level, which take as much of the triggering damage as possible. Immediately after you take the damage, you gain vulnerability to fire damage, your speed reduces to 0ft., and you are incapacitated. These effects end when the ice melts, along with the temporary hitpoints. You may use this invocation once every short rest.

content.name: Undying Servitude
content.desc: You may cast the @animate dead spell without expending a spell slot, once every long rest.

6.name: Otherworldly Patron Feature
6.desc: You gain a feature depending on your patron.

7.name: Eldritch Invocations
7.desc: You gain an additional eldritch invocation, for a total of 4. The following invocations become available to you:

content.name: Bewitching Whispers
content.desc: You may cast the @compulsion spell using a warlock spell slot, once every long rest.

content.name: Dreadful Word
content.desc: You may cast the @confusion spell using a warlock spell slot, once every long rest.

content.name: Ghostly Gaze
content.desc: As an action, you may touch a willing humanoid and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. As long as the target is on the same plane of existence as you, you may use your action on subsequent turns to maintain this connection, extending the duration until the end of your next turn every time. While perceiving through the other creature's senses, you benefit from any special senses possessed by that creature, and you are blinded and deafened to your own surroundings.

content.name: Protection of the Talisman
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the talisman. Whenever the wearer of your talisman fails a saving throw, they may add 1d4 to the roll, potentially turning the save into a success. This benefit may be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus every long rest.

content.name: Relentless Hex
content.desc: Prerequisite: the @hex spell, or a warlock feature that curses. Your curse creates a temporary bond between you and the target. As a bonus action, you may magically teleport up to 30ft. to an unoccupied space you can see within 5ft. of the cursed target, provided you can see the target.

content.name: Sculptor of Flesh
content.desc: You may cast the @polymorph spell using a warlock spell slot, once every long rest.

content.name: Trickster's Escape
content.desc: You may cast the @freedom of movement spell on yourself without expending a spell slot, once every long rest.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

9.name: Eldritch Invocations
9.desc: You gain an additional eldritch invocation, for a total of 5. The following invocations become available to you:

content.name: Ascendant Step
content.desc: You may cast the @levitate spell on yourself at will without expending a spell slot, or needing material components.

content.name: Gift of the Protectors
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the tome. A new page appears in your book of shadows. With your permission, a creature may use its action to write its name on that page, up to a number of names equal to your proficiency bonus. Whenever a creature whose name is on the page is reduced to 0 hitpoints, but not killed outright, the creature drops to 1 hitpoint instead. Once this is triggered, no creature may benefit from it until you finish a long rest. You may erase a name on the page as an action, by touching it.

content.name: Minions of Chaos
content.desc: You may cast the @conjure elemental spell using a warlock spell slot, once every long rest.

content.name: Otherworldy Leap
content.desc: You may cast the @jump spell at will without expending a spell slot.

content.name: Whispers of the Grave
content.desc: You may cast the @speak with dead spell at will without expending a spell slot.

10.name: Otherworldly Patron Feature
10.desc: You gain a feature depending on your patron.

10.name: Cantrips
10.desc: You learn an additional cantrip, for a total of 3.

11.name: Mystic Arcanum
11.desc: Your patron grants you a 6th-level arcanum. You may choose 1 6th-level spell from the warlock spell list and cast it once every long rest. You may change the spell during this long rest.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

12.name: Eldritch Invocations
12.desc: You gain an additional eldritch invocation, for a total of 6. The following invocations become available to you:

content.name: Bond of the Talisman
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the talisman. While someone else is wearing your talisman, you may use your action to teleport to the unoccupied space closest to them, provided the two of you are on the same plane of existence. The wearer of your talisman can do the same thing to teleport to you. Teleportation in this way can be done a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus every long rest.

content.name: Lifedrinker
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the blade. Whenever you hit a creature with your pact weapon, the target takes bonus necrotic damage equal to your CHA (minimum of 1).

13.name: Mystic Arcanum
13.desc: Your patron grants you a 7th-level arcanum. You may choose 1 7th-level spell from the warlock spell list and cast it once every long rest. You may change the spell during this long rest.

14.name: Otherworldly Patron Feature
14.desc: You gain a feature depending on your patron.

15.name: Mystic Arcanum
15.desc: Your patron grants you a 8th-level arcanum. You may choose 1 8th-level spell from the warlock spell list and cast it once every long rest. You may change the spell during this long rest.

15.name: Eldritch Invocations
15.desc: You gain an additional eldritch invocation, for a total of 7. The following invocations become available to you:

content.name: Chains of Carceri
content.desc: Prerequisite: pact of the chain. You may cast the @hold monster spell at will, targeting a celestial, fiend, or elemental, without expending a spell slot or needing material components. You may affect every creature with this invocation once every long rest.

content.name: Master of Myriad Forms
content.desc: You may cast the @alter self spell at will without expending a spell slot.

content.name: Shroud of Shadow
content.desc: You may cast the @invisibilty spell at will without expending a spell slot.

content.name: Visions of Distant Realms
content.desc: You may cast the @arcane eye spell at will without expending a spell slot.

content.name: Witch Sight
content.desc: You are able to see the true form of any shapechanger or creature concealed by illusion or transmutation magic, while the creature is within 30ft. of you and within line of sight.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

17.name: Mystic Arcanum
17.desc: Your patron grants you a 9th-level arcanum. You may choose 1 9th-level spell from the warlock spell list and cast it once every long rest. You may change the spell during this long rest.

18.name: Eldritch Invocations
18.desc: You gain an additional eldritch invocation, for a total of 8.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

20.name: Eldritch Master
20.desc: Once every long rest, you may spend 1 minute entreating your patron to regain all your expended spell slots.

wizard.name: Wizard
desc: Wizards are supreme magic-users, defined and united as a class by the spells they cast. Drawing on the subtle weave of magic that permeates the cosmos, wizards cast spells of explosive fire, arcing lightning, subtle deception, brute-force mind control, and much more.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.desc: As a student of arcane magic, you have a spellbook containing spells that show the first glimmerings of your true power.

content.name: Cantrips
content.desc: You know 3 cantrips of your choice from the wizard spell list.

content.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
content.desc: You may prepare a number of wizard spells equal to your INT + your wizard level, minimum of 1 spell. The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. You may change any number of spells in your spell list during a long rest, requiring 1 minute of meditation per spell level for each spell you change. The spells you prepare must be in your spellbook.

content.name: Spellbook
content.desc: You have a spellbook that contains 4 + 2 * your wizard level wizard spells of your choice. These spells can only be of spell levels you can prepare. Your spellbook is the repository of the wizard spells you know, except your cantrips, which are fixed in your mind. The spells you add to your spellbook as you gain levels reflect the arcane research you conduct on your own, as well as intellectual breakthroughs that you have had about the nature of the multiverse. You might find other spells during your adventures. You could discover a spell recorded on a scroll in an evil wizard's chest, for example, or in a dusty tome in an ancient library.

content.name: Copying a Spell Into the Book
content.desc: When you find a wizard spell of 1st level or higher, you may add it to your spellbook if it is of a spell level you can prepare, and if you can spare the time to decipher and copy it. Copying a spell into your spellbook involves reproducing the basic form of the spell, the deciphering the unique system of notation used by the wizard who wrote it. You must practice the spell until you understand the sounds or gestures required, then transcribe it into your spellbook using your own notation. For each level of the spell, the process takes 2 hours, and costs 50gp. The cost represents material components you expend as you experiment with the spell to master it, as well as the fine inks you need to record it. Once you have spent this time and money, you can prepare the spell just like your other spells.

content.name: Replacing the Book
content.desc: You may copy a spell from your own spellbook into another book. For example, if you want to make a backup copy of your spellbook. This works the same way as copying a new spell into your spellbook, but you need spend only 1 hour and 10gp per spell level.

content.name: Ritual Casting
content.desc: You may cast any wizard spell you have prepared as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag.

content.name: Spellcasting Focus
content.desc: You may use an arcane focus as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.

content.name: Spellcasting Ability
content.desc: Your spellcasting ability is INT.

content.name: Spell Save DC
content.desc: 8 + proficiency + INT

content.name: Spell attack modifier
content.desc: proficiency + INT

1.name: Arcane Recovery
1.desc: You have learned to regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. Once per day, after finishing a short rest, you may choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your wizard level (rounded up), and none of the spell slots can be 6th level or higher.

2.name: Arcane Tradition
2.desc: Choose a subclass:

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.desc: You may increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1. Optionally, you may forego this improvement and choose a feat instead.

4.name: Cantrips

You may cast these two spells at their lowest level without expending a spell slot when you have them prepared. If you want to cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot as usual. You may exchange one or both spells chosen by spending 8 hours in study.

20.desc: You gain mastery over 2 powerful spells, and can cast them with little effort. Choose 2 3rd-level wizard spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared (they don't count against the number of spells you have prepared) and you can cast each of them once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot. You may cast both spells once per long rest. If you want to cast either spell at a higher level, you must expend a spell slot as usual.
